name: Pocono Medical Center
description_short: Federal credit union in East Stroudsburg, PA serving the Pocono Medical Center area since 1973 with one branch location and financial services.
description_long:
Pocono Medical Center Federal Credit Union was established in 1973 and has grown to serve the East Stroudsburg, Pennsylvania area. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates under NCUA oversight and maintains a mission to make quality financial services accessible to its membership. The institution has expanded its reach across the greater Pocono region over its 50+ year operating history.

The credit union operates a single main branch location at 206 East Brown Street in East Stroudsburg, PA 18301, with lobby hours from 9:00 am to 3:30 pm on business days. Members have access to branch services, ATM networks, and location-based tools including branch locators and nearby ATM finders. The institution provides standard financial services typical of credit unions, though specific product details are not detailed on the CreditDoc reference page.

As a member-owned credit union rather than a for-profit bank, Pocono Medical Center FCU operates with a focus on serving its specific membership base in the medical center community. The organization has maintained continuity since 1973 and provides the infrastructure of branch banking, ATM access, and member support services. Its single-location footprint reflects its local, community-focused mission.

This is a small, regional credit union with limited physical presence—one branch in a specific geographic area means access is restricted to those near East Stroudsburg or willing to travel. The website provides basic locator information but limited details on specific loan products, rates, or competitive advantages. Prospective members should contact the institution directly at (570) 476-3338 or visit pmcfcu.org for detailed product offerings and membership eligibility requirements. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Branch banking services at main office location
ATM network access and cash withdrawal services
Member financial accounts (specific types not detailed on website)
pros: Federally chartered credit union with NCUA insurance protection for deposits
Over 50 years of operating history (founded 1973) demonstrating stability
Member-owned structure typically offers better rates and lower fees than commercial banks
Convenient branch location with posted lobby hours (9:00 am - 3:30 pm)
Interactive branch locator and ATM finder tools available on website
Accessible phone support at (570) 476-3338 during business hours
cons: Only one physical branch location limits accessibility for members outside East Stroudsburg
Website provides minimal information about specific loan products, rates, or service details
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital services capabilities
Limited geographic footprint restricts membership to those with local ties or willingness to travel
No information about competitive advantages compared to larger regional or national institutions
best_for: Pocono Medical Center employees and healthcare system staff seeking employer-affiliated banking
East Stroudsburg, PA residents needing local community bank relationships and branch access
Individuals prioritizing credit union membership benefits over national bank conveniences
diagnosis: Pocono Medical Center Federal Credit Union is best suited for employees of the Pocono Medical Center system and residents of East Stroudsburg, PA seeking community-based credit union banking with NCUA protections. The primary caveat is its single-branch limitation and lack of detailed online information—prospective members must contact the institution directly to understand specific products, rates, and membership eligibility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
